topic: Choosing documentation patterns for an internal developer platform
reader: platform lead preparing a scoped decision memo
use_context: A short decision memo that helps the team decide when to emphasize ADRs, runbooks, and interface contracts.
question: How should the team divide responsibility among ADRs, runbooks, and interface contracts without overclaiming that one format replaces the others?

source_packets.title: Architecture practice guide
source_packets.summary: Explains when architecture decision records add durable value.

findings.statement: ADRs are most useful when the team needs a durable record of why a technical choice was made and what alternatives were rejected.
findings.decision_note: Use ADRs for durable architectural choices, not for volatile operating detail.
failure_modes: Using ADRs as a catch-all document type

findings.statement: Interface contracts work best when teams need a shared, reviewable description of data shapes and behavioral expectations across boundaries.
findings.decision_note: Treat interface contracts as boundary documents rather than architectural history.
failure_modes: Mixing interface obligations into narrative architecture notes

source_packets.title: Operations handbook excerpt
source_packets.summary: Shows how runbooks reduce recovery ambiguity when systems fail.

findings.statement: Runbooks are most valuable when responders need time-sensitive operating steps, escalation paths, and rollback guidance.
findings.decision_note: Use runbooks for live operation and recovery, not as a substitute for architectural reasoning.
failure_modes: Treating a narrative design note as an incident guide
misunderstandings: Assuming responders can infer recovery steps from design documents

source_packets.title: Scoped documentation inventory
source_packets.summary: A local inventory of the current document set used in the team.

findings.statement: Within the scoped source set, no single document type appears to replace ADRs, runbooks, and interface contracts across every use case.
findings.decision_note: Keep this framed as a scoped observation rather than a universal rule.
failure_modes: Turning a scoped inventory result into a universal claim
misunderstandings: Assuming the inventory covers every team or future workflow
findings.caveat: This finding is limited to the current source set.
